paper_focus.title: Making low alloy ultrahigh strength steel tough by high-density NiAl clusters
paper_focus.research_objective: To develop a low alloy ultrahigh strength steel with dual precipitation of high-density NiAl clusters and ε-carbides, and to elucidate the nucleation mechanisms, dislocation regulation, and multi-scale toughening mechanisms that enable high toughness at ~1.9 GPa tensile strength.
material_systems: 1.3Al steel (modified with 1.30% Al and higher Ni: Fe-0.31C-2.01Si-1.03Mn-1.00Cr-5.06Ni-0.60Mo-0.11V-1.30Al, wt%)
B2-NiAl nanoclusters (high number density ~4.88×10^24 m−3, lattice misfit <3% with α-Fe)
martensitic matrix (lath martensite with retained austenite)
main_process_variables: composition: Al content 0.03 wt% vs 1.30 wt%; Ni content 3.41 wt% vs 5.06 wt%
solution treatment: 970°C for 1 h, water quench
tensile test: room temperature, strain rate 0.001 s−1, specimen gauge length 25 mm, diameter 5 mm
Charpy U-notch impact test (KU2): specimen 10×10×55 mm3
plane strain fracture toughness (three-point bending): specimen thickness 25 mm, width 50 mm, 3 mm fatigue pre-crack, loading rate 38 kN/min, KQ per ASTM E399
expected_information_axis: microstructure (martensite laths, dislocation density/distribution, precipitate size/number density)
precipitation behavior (NiAl cluster nucleation, ε-carbide formation, Si interaction)
mechanical properties (tensile strength, elongation, impact energy, fracture toughness)
fracture analysis (dimple morphology, crack deflection, secondary cracks, void coalescence)
toughening mechanisms (dislocation homogenization, strain delocalization, energy dissipation)
